---
title: "The Rev. Charles Alexander Johns"
---

## The Rev. Charles Alexander Johns

**Dates:** (1811-1874)

### Description

naturalist

### Biography

He taught at Helston Grammar School, when Derwent Coleridge was headmaster, and later ran a prep school at Winton House, Winchester.  He was founder and president of the Winchester Literary and Scientific Society.  He married (1843) Ellen Julia Field (b. Bath 1821/2) Beenham in 1851 census, Watford in 1861.  His father was a Plymouth banker, whose firm had collapsed in 1825. His most famous book was the very popular <i>Flowers of the Field</i> (1851). [ODNB]

### Letters

- Letter from Oct 22nd [1879?]
- Letter from July 17th 1879
- Letter from Feb 1st [endorsed \'1872\']
- Letter from [1870-2?][[footnote:1]]
- Letter from September 28 [1867]
- Letter from July 30th [1867]
- Letter from July 6th [1867]
- Letter from Jan 30th [1867] 
- Letter from Decr 22d [1865]	
- Letter from July 25th 1865
- Letter from July 1st 1865

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
